response:
Sec. 1  
Allergies to peanuts are immune system responses to proteins found in peanuts  
These proteins trigger reactions such as hives swelling or difficulty breathing  
Living underwater does not eliminate exposure to peanut proteins  
People with peanut allergies can still consume peanuts or come into contact with them  
Underwater environments do not provide protection from allergens  
Allergic reactions are not influenced by the presence of water  

Sec. 2  
The body's immune response to allergens is not affected by external conditions such as water  
Allergies are determined by genetic and immunological factors  
Living underwater does not change the way the body reacts to allergens  
People with peanut allergies must avoid allergens regardless of their environment  
The inability to live underwater is unrelated to peanut allergies  
Therefore peanut allergies do not prevent people from living underwater
